content.full_text:
June 25, 1990

Mr. John Wallace  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear John:

On May 15, 1990, I spoke to you regarding fundraisers held jointly for two or more candidates. I would like to confirm in writing the telephone advice you provided to me.

Question: Can an entity, such as an informal group of persons ("The Group"), organize a fundraising event for two or more candidates, at the behest of the candidates, without becoming a recipient committee pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(a)?

I understand that your answer to the above question was affirmative under the following conditions:

1. The Group creates a "trust-type" account to receive all contributions and from which all receipts are distributed to the candidates;

2. All contributions are earmarked by the contributors for the specific candidates in permissible amounts in writing;

3. All records of contributors of $25 or more are maintained as though there was a reporting requirement;

4. The Group reports the costs to each candidate on a pro-rata basis as an in-kind contribution;

5. The Group is an intermediary for all contributions; and

6. Written solicitation must conform to Regulation 18523.1.

Also, you indicated that it might be possible to pay for the costs of the fundraising from contribution receipts, but

Mr. John Wallace  
June 25, 1990  
Page Two

that such a procedure would greatly complicate the handling of funds and recordkeeping and reporting requirements.

I would appreciate your reply. If you have any questions, do not hesitate to contact me at 446-6752.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
BRUCE W. ROBECK  
Paralegal Administrator

BWR:kan
